Title: E9-S1 Procure WhatsApp Business number (Twilio preferred, Wati alt)
Description: Sign up with Twilio for the WhatsApp Business API. Use their sandbox phone-number first for demo testing — production number requires Meta business verification (1-2 weeks). Decision: Twilio for setup speed; switch to Wati / Gupshup later only if India-billing or BSP handholding becomes blocking.

Title: E9-S2 WhatsApp adapter — webhook + send-message helpers
Description: Add `services/whatsappService.ts` mirroring `services/telegramService.ts` (sendMessage, downloadMedia, secret validation, status callbacks). Add `app/api/webhooks/whatsapp/route.ts` to receive Twilio inbound webhooks. Persist normalised messages into the existing `channel_messages` table with `channel='whatsapp'`.

Title: E9-S3 Plug WhatsApp adapter into intakeConversationManager
Description: Reuse the V2 LLM pipeline — it's already channel-agnostic. Conversation history persists in `channel_conversations.metadata_json.history[]` with the same shape as Telegram. Civic-scope filter + multilingual support work without changes.

Title: E9-S4 Message template approvals (Meta-mandatory)
Description: Twilio requires Meta-approved templates for any outbound message sent OUTSIDE the 24-hour customer-care window. Submit templates for: ticket-created, ticket-accepted, citizen-contacted, resolved, awaiting-response. Each in English + Telugu. Review cycle is 1-3 days per template.

Title: E9-S5 Initial demo testing on real WhatsApp numbers
Description: Internal team uses the Twilio sandbox / demo number to file test tickets end-to-end. Validate: text intake, voice note attachment, image attachment, multi-turn conversation, civic-scope decline, status check.

Title: E9-S6 Production cutover decision
Description: Decide: run WhatsApp + Telegram in parallel forever, or sunset Telegram once WhatsApp is proven? Capture decision in PRD §17. Most likely: parallel — different demographics use different apps.

Title: E10-S1 Product discovery — citizen app MVP scope
Description: What does the citizen app do that Telegram cannot? Photo capture (better than Telegram inline), offline draft (save when no network, sync when back online), push status notifications, map-based location pinning, history of all my tickets. Output: 1-page brief + low-fi wireframes.

Title: E10-S2 Tech-stack decision (React Native / Flutter / PWA)
Description: React Native is the safe bet — shares JS with our Next.js team, mature ecosystem, cross-platform. Flutter is a fresh codebase + Dart skill requirement. PWA avoids app stores but worse on push notifications. Decision lands once discovery output is in hand.

Title: E10-S3 Citizen auth — phone OTP
Description: Currently citizens have no app-level auth — they're identified by Telegram chat_id. App needs real auth. Clerk supports phone OTP out of the box. Bind the resulting Clerk user to the same `citizens` table row that the Telegram bot would otherwise create.

Title: E10-S4 API surface for citizen actions
Description: New `/api/citizen/*` routes (auth via citizen Clerk JWT, NOT staff): POST file-ticket, POST attach-media, GET status (for one ticket), GET my-tickets (list), GET notifications. Separate from `/api/admin/*` and the worker routes.

Title: E10-S5 MVP build — issue reporting + status check
Description: Two screens: New Issue (form + photo + voice + location pin), My Tickets (list with status badges, tap into detail). Polished but minimal — no chat-style follow-ups for V1 (the LLM intake conversation manager stays Telegram/WhatsApp only).

Title: E10-S6 App store + Play store submission
Description: Privacy policy, store listing copy, app icon, screenshots, age rating. First-approval reviews are typically 1-2 weeks. Plan for at least one rejection-and-resubmit cycle on each store.

Title: E10-S7 Initial cohort rollout
Description: Same staged-rollout pattern as the Telegram bot: 50 users → 500 → general availability. Each cohort gets a feedback survey embedded in the app.

Title: E11-S1 Report an issue CTA on the worker dashboard
Description: Floating action button on `/my-assignments`. Tap opens a new-issue modal. Available to ground_worker, district_leader, central_support. Hidden for other roles.

Title: E11-S2 New-issue form — title / description / location / category / photos
Description: Reuse the existing ticket creation service (`services/ticketService.createTicket`). Distinction: `source_channel = 'worker_report'` on the ticket so reports/triage can filter on it. Photos go through E1's storage pipeline.

Title: E11-S3 API: POST /api/tickets/worker-report
Description: Role-gated to ground_worker / district_leader / central_support. Sets `created_by` to the worker. Forces `needs_triage = true` (do NOT auto-assign back to the reporter — conflict of interest). Emits `ticket_created_by_worker` audit row.

Title: E11-S4 Triage queue — worker-reported badge
Description: Small badge in `/triage` so central support can spot worker-sourced reports. These often have higher signal-to-noise than citizen reports (workers are on the ground). Could rank-bump them.

Title: E11-S5 Audit + access-control — worker can't self-assign their own report
Description: Enforce at the API layer for /api/tickets/assign + /api/tickets/auto-assign — reject if the assignee is the same user who reported. UI: hide the reporter from the assignment dropdown for their own ticket.

Title: E11-S6 Reporting — worker-sourced vs citizen-sourced split
Description: KPI tile on `/reports`: % of tickets sourced from workers vs citizens. Trend over time. Tells leadership how proactive the ground team is.

Title: E12-S1 Migration 007 — add sla_meeting_hours + sla_breach_alerts_enabled
Description: ALTER TABLE organization_settings ADD COLUMN sla_meeting_hours int NOT NULL DEFAULT 12; ADD COLUMN sla_breach_alerts_enabled boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT true. Existing rows backfill from defaults. Same idempotent style as migration 006.

Title: E12-S2 Plumb sla_meeting_due_at through ticket-acceptance + reports
Description: When a worker accepts a ticket (`/api/tickets/accept`), compute `sla_meeting_due_at` alongside `sla_first_contact_due_at` and `sla_resolution_due_at` using the new column. Show the meeting countdown on the worker UI. Add to ticket detail SLA bar.

Title: E12-S3 SuperAdmin SLA-settings UI page
Description: New `/admin/sla-settings` page (super_admin only). Number inputs for each SLA (acceptance min, first-contact h, meeting h, resolution h, max attempts). Validation: each must be >= the previous step. Audit log on every save. Mirrors `/admin/intake-settings` pattern.

Title: E12-S4 Per-category SLA overrides
Description: Critical severity gets tighter SLAs than Low. Either a JSON column (`sla_overrides_json`) on organization_settings, or a separate per-category table. Defer until we have first-launch data on which mismatches actually matter.

Title: E12-S5 SLA breach behaviour config — escalation + alert recipients
Description: Currently breach sets `sla_breached_flag` and that's it. Extend: notify configurable list (state_leader, district_leader, escalation Telegram channel). Behaviour gated by the new `sla_breach_alerts_enabled` flag.
